summary: n8n-nodes-final-mile exfiltrates Kubernetes credentials and opens a reverse shell to a hardcoded C2 on require
details: The package masquerades as an n8n community node but ships an IIFE in index.js that executes on require(). On load it reads ~/.kube/config from /home/ubuntu and /root, invokes kubectl to enumerate pods, services, nodes, namespaces, and secrets across all namespaces, and sends the collected cluster data over a raw TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8890. It also spawns /bin/bash and wires an interactive shell to 103.27.109.184:8895 via /dev/tcp, granting full remote command execution on the installer's host. There is no legitimate n8n node code in the package; the entire payload is credential theft and a backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
